message.content:
Adversarially verify this finding about the repo at /home/jleechan/projects/ez-gh-actions (public: github.com/jleechanorg/ez-gh-actions, evidence at /home/jleechan/projects/ez-gh-actions/evidence/e2e-20260703, run https://github.com/jleechanorg/ez-gh-actions/actions/runs/28685531107). Try to REFUTE it by reading the actual code/evidence and, where relevant, live gh queries. Finding from "code-bugs" (critical): stop_all() deregisters OTHER hosts' ezgha runners — org/multi-host `ezgha stop` is a fleet-wide outage button
docker_backend.rs:148-154. After removing local containers, `stop_all` lists ALL runners on the target and removes every one whose name starts with `ezgha-` and `!r.busy`. Runner names are `ezgha-{hostname}-{suffix}` (docker_backend.rs:67), so with org scope (or a shared repo) host A's `ezgha stop` deregisters host B's and host C's idle runners too. Concrete scenario: 3 hosts each serving `count=2` against org `jleechanorg`; operator runs `ezgha stop` on host A to take it down for maintenance → all 6 idle registrations are deleted; hosts B/C's waiting listeners are severed server-side, their containers exit, and queued jobs sit with zero available runners until B/C's 30s serve loops re-register. Repeated stops cause continuous org-wide runner churn. Additionally the busy check is a stale snapshot: a runner can pick up a job between list_runners() and remove_runner() (GitHub returns 422 for busy runners, which mitigates but produces spurious errors that are silently swallowed by `let _ =`).
Refute if the factual basis is wrong, the scenario cannot occur, or severity is inflated (severity inflation alone -> not refuted, but say so).

input.description: Read evidence config and status captures

content.content:
version = 1

[github]
scope = "repo"
target = "jleechanorg/ez-gh-actions"

[runner]
labels = [
    "self-hosted",
    "ezgha",
]
count = 1
image = "ghcr.io/actions/actions-runner:latest"

[limits]
memory_mb = 5977
cpus = 2.0
pids = 512
min_free_disk_gb = 10

[policy]
minimum_isolation = "container"
managed containers: 1
  1143cf62e74f ezgha-Jeff-Ubuntu-6a4833a11c652b (running, up 21 seconds ago)
registered ezgha runners on jleechanorg/ez-gh-actions: 1
  #3 ezgha-Jeff-Ubuntu-6a4833a11c652b status=online busy=false
managed containers: 0
registered ezgha runners on jleechanorg/ez-gh-actions: 0

input.command: gh api orgs/jleechanorg/actions/runners?per_page=100 --jq '{total: .total_count, runners: [.runners[] | {id, name, status, busy}]}' 2>&1 | head -30; echo ---; gh api repos/jleechanorg/ez-gh-actions/actions/runners?per_page=100 --jq '{total: .total_count, runners: [.runners[] | {id, name, status, busy}]}' 2>&1 | head -30
input.description: List org-level and repo-level self-hosted runners via GitHub API

input.reasoning: Verified against actual code, evidence bundle, and live gh API. (1) docker_backend.rs:141-156 stop_all removes every runner named ezgha-* with !busy from the configured target with no hostname scoping, despite names embedding hostname (line 67) — cited lines exact. (2) github.rs api_base maps org scope to orgs/{target}, so list/remove operate org-wide. (3) Live query of orgs/jleechanorg/actions/runners returned 21 runners spanning multiple physical hosts in one flat list, confirming host A sees and can delete host B/C registrations at org scope. (4) Evidence file 04_status_before_job.txt shows a real idle JIT runner listed as busy=false, confirming idle JIT registrations match the deletion predicate. (5) main.rs:210 confirms the 30s serve loop; hosts running one-shot `ezgha start` never self-heal, making the impact potentially worse than stated. (6) The TOCTOU busy-snapshot claim is accurate and the 422 is swallowed by `let _ =` at line 151. Minor severity nuance: in serve mode the outage is transient (~30s, jobs queue rather than fail), so "outage button" is worst-case framing — but severity inflation alone does not refute, and the unsupervised-start case is persistent capacity loss. Factual basis fully confirmed; not refuted.
